one_liner: CELH up 5.8% in Wyckoff Accumulation Phase A-B with 53% DCF upside to $45.79 — multiple analyst buy ratings maintained despite lowered targets; energy drink market share recovery thesis intact.

news_catalyst.headline: Multiple Analysts Maintain Buy on Celsius Holdings — Roth Capital, B of A, Morgan Stanley All Bullish Despite Lower Targets
news_catalyst.summary: Three major banks (Roth Capital, B of A Securities, Morgan Stanley) all maintained Buy/Overweight ratings on CELH today while lowering price targets to $45-57, all well above the current price of $29.85. Reddit enthusiasm for CELH was highlighted by 24/7 Wall St., suggesting retail accumulation is accompanying institutional interest. At $29.85, the stock trades at 53% discount to the DCF base case of $45.79 — a compelling value entry in the energy drink space.

technical.summary: CELH is in Wyckoff Accumulation Phase A-B with 2/5 signals detected and bullish bias. RSI at 50 is neutral — plenty of room to run. MACD in bullish crossover. The stock is building a base between $27.74 support and $36.24 resistance. A break above $36 on elevated volume would confirm Phase C entry and signal accumulation phase completion.

valuation.summary: 53% DCF upside to base case of $45.79 with 23% growth rate — reflects recovery in energy drink market share. Bear case of $23 is 23% downside, making risk/reward approximately 2.3:1 in favor of longs at current price.

key_strengths: Grade A fundamentals — top-tier for consumer staples
23% growth rate in competitive energy drink market
Strong brand loyalty and distribution expansion
key_concerns: PepsiCo distribution partnership risk if relationship sours
Premium positioning under pressure in discretionary spending slowdown
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with 23% projected growth — outstanding for a consumer products company. Multiple institutional buy ratings with targets averaging $50 confirm the fundamental case. The selldown from $43 high to $30 appears overdone.

risks: Consumer discretionary spending slowdown per Chewy guidance — energy drinks are semi-discretionary
Energy drink market competition intensifying (Monster, Prime, Alani Nu)
Bear case DCF at $22.95 — 23% downside if growth disappoints
